topic: LLM Session Compaction and Conversation Memory

sources.title: Anthropic Compaction — Server-Side Context Compaction (Official Docs)
keyInsights: Server-side compaction is in beta (header: compact-2026-01-12), supported on Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6
Trigger threshold: 150,000 input tokens by default; minimum 50,000 tokens
Default summarization prompt: 'Write down anything that would be helpful, including the state, next steps, learnings etc.'
API returns a 'compaction' block at the start of the assistant response; all prior content blocks are dropped on next request
pause_after_compaction=true allows injecting verbatim messages after the summary before continuing
Custom summarization instructions completely replace (not supplement) the default prompt
Token counting: top-level input_tokens/output_tokens do NOT include compaction iteration usage; must sum usage.iterations[]
Compaction blocks can have cache_control set for prompt caching optimization
Same model is used for summarization — no option to use a cheaper model
context_rot: as context length grows, model accuracy and recall degrade; compaction keeps context focused

codeExamples.description: Streaming compaction with compaction block detection
Total token budget enforcement via compaction counter

sources.title: Anthropic Context Windows Guide (Official Docs)
keyInsights: Context rot: model accuracy and recall degrade as context window fills, independent of context window size
Claude Sonnet/Haiku 4.5 have context awareness: receive token budget updates after each tool call via <system_warning>
Progressive token accumulation: each turn preserves full previous turns, growing linearly
Extended thinking blocks are automatically stripped from subsequent turns by the API
Validation error (not silent truncation) when tokens exceed context window on newer models
Server-side compaction recommended as primary strategy for long-running conversations
context_management.edits API for compaction; context_editing API for tool result/thinking block clearing

sources.title: Anthropic Engineering: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents
keyInsights: Context engineering: strategic curation of tokens for LLM inference — the optimal set, not the maximum set
Context rot is caused by finite attention budget — n^2 token relationships in transformer architecture
Compaction strategy: summarize conversation history to reset context while preserving key decisions, unresolved issues, implementation details
Structured note-taking: persistent external memory (CLAUDE.md, task lists) retrieved when needed
Sub-agent architectures: specialized agents return 1000-2000 token condensed summaries to coordinator
Goal: find the smallest set of high-signal tokens that maximize the likelihood of the desired outcome

sources.title: Pinecone: LangChain Conversational Memory Types
keyInsights: ConversationBufferMemory: stores complete unmodified history; simple but grows linearly with conversation
ConversationSummaryMemory: LLM progressively summarizes conversation; slows token growth but requires extra LLM calls
ConversationSummaryBufferMemory: hybrid — recent exchanges verbatim + summarized older history; max_token_limit parameter
Buffer memory best for short conversations; summary memory best for long ones needing distant context
Hybrid (SummaryBuffer) competitive on token usage despite dual storage
ConversationTokenBufferMemory: sliding window by token count (not message count)

sources.title: LangGraph Memory Documentation
keyInsights: In-thread memory: checkpointed state within single conversation session
Cross-thread memory: custom namespace JSON document store for cross-session persistence
Three memory categories: semantic (facts/entities), episodic (past actions), procedural (rules/instructions)
Hot path updates: memories created during runtime (real-time but adds latency)
Background updates: async memory formation without blocking primary application
Storage: JSON documents with hierarchical namespace-key structure supporting semantic search

sources.title: LangChain Blog: Long-Term Memory in LangGraph
keyInsights: Cross-thread memory enables agents to store and recall information between conversations
Simple document store primitive: put, get, search operations
Custom namespace organization (per user, organization, context)
No universally perfect solution — application-specific logic required
Addresses the 'goldfish problem': most AI apps forget everything between conversations

sources.title: LangChain Blog: Memory for Agents
keyInsights: Three memory types mapped to human psychology: procedural (LLM weights + code), semantic (long-term facts), episodic (past events)
Semantic memory: extract from conversations, retrieve to personalize future responses
Episodic memory: few-shot example prompting to guide agent behavior
Memory update hot path: agent decides via tool call before responding (adds latency)
Memory update background: separate process updates without blocking (not immediate)
Memory is fundamentally application-specific — no one-size-fits-all solution

sources.title: Zep Documentation — Context Engineering Platform
keyInsights: Zep is a 'context engineering platform' — assembles personalized context via agent memory, Graph RAG, and context assembly
Conversation threading: structured management across sessions with context retrieval within branches
Fact extraction: precise, time-stamped information capturing relationships about specific events
Entity summaries: contextualized entity histories that update with new information (evolving profiles)
Temporal knowledge graphs: entities and relationships tracked over time
Custom extraction instructions for domain-specific optimization
Context templates: formatted retrieval for customized presentation

sources.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems (arxiv)
keyInsights: Virtual context management inspired by OS hierarchical memory systems
Intelligently manages multiple memory layers to extend usable context beyond native limits
OS-like paging: moves data between fast (context window) and slow (external) storage
Control flow interrupts to manage interactions with the user
Effective for document analysis (beyond context window) and multi-session chat
Agents can 'remember, reflect, and evolve' through sustained user interactions

sources.title: Letta (MemGPT) Agent Memory Documentation
keyInsights: Core memory blocks: injected into LLM context window; agent can modify via memory tools
Memory blocks can be attached to multiple agents simultaneously (shared blocks)
All state (memories, messages, tool calls, reasoning) persisted to database — never lost on eviction
Context window eviction does not equal permanent loss — storage layer is the ground truth
Agent-editable memory enables self-modification of working memory

sources.title: From Sparse to Dense: GPT-4 Summarization with Chain of Density (arxiv)
keyInsights: Chain of Density (CoD): iteratively generates denser summaries by inserting missing entities without expanding length
Starts with an entity-sparse summary; each iteration adds salient overlooked entities
Fixed length constraint forces meaningful compression and reordering
CoD summaries are more abstract, more fused, less dependent on source document ordering
Fundamental tradeoff: informativeness vs readability — humans preferred denser CoD output
Progressive refinement technique applicable to conversation compression

sources.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior (arxiv)
keyInsights: Memory stream: complete record of experiences in natural language
Hierarchical summarization: raw memories synthesized into higher-level reflections
Dynamic retrieval: relevance + recency + importance scoring for memory access
Three critical components (ablation-verified): Observation, Planning, Reflection
Reflection: synthesizes experiences into insights over time
Emergent social behaviors from memory-aware agent interactions

sources.title: A Simple Yet Strong Baseline for Long-Term Conversational Memory (arxiv)
keyInsights: Event-centric propositions: represent history as self-contained elementary discourse units (EDUs)
Heterogeneous graph structure for associative recall across sessions
Retrieval: dense similarity search with LLM filtering + optional graph-based propagation
Matches or surpasses baselines while operating with shorter QA contexts
Source turn attribution preserved in each EDU

sources.title: Structured Distillation for Personalized Agent Memory (arxiv)
keyInsights: 11x token compression: reduces average exchange from 371 to 38 tokens
Four structured fields: exchange_core, specific_context, thematic room_assignments, regex-extracted files_touched
Tested on 4182 conversations from 6 software engineering projects
96% of best verbatim MRR (0.717 vs 0.745) with best configuration
Vector search: no significant degradation. BM25: significant degradation
Enables thousands of exchanges to fit within a single prompt

sources.title: HiMeS: Hippocampus-inspired Memory System for LLM Agents (arxiv)
keyInsights: Dual-memory architecture: short-term memory extractor + partitioned long-term memory network
RL-trained short-term compression: distills recent conversations while pre-retrieving relevant docs
Emulates hippocampus-prefrontal cortex interaction from neuroscience
Long-term component handles user-specific info + document re-ranking
Reduces redundant clarification, irrelevant documents, degraded user experience
Tested on real-world industrial datasets with significant QA quality improvements

sources.title: Aeon: High-Performance Neuro-Symbolic Memory Management (arxiv)
keyInsights: Cognitive operating system: redesigns memory as a managed resource
Spatial indexing + episodic graphs for 3.1x spatial compression
Neuro-symbolic approach: combines neural retrieval with symbolic memory management

sources.title: Cache What Lasts: TRIM-KV Token Retention for Memory-Bounded KV Cache (arxiv)
keyInsights: Retention gates: lightweight per-token scalar importance scores that decay over time
Learns importance at creation time — not retroactively
Evicts tokens below threshold when memory limit is reached
Surpasses full-cache models in some settings: selective retention as regularization
Naturally recovers sink tokens and sliding window patterns without explicit design

sources.title: Adaptive Focus Memory (AFM) for Language Models (arxiv)
keyInsights: Three fidelity levels: Full, Compressed, Placeholder — assigned per message
Assignment factors: semantic relevance to current dialogue, temporal decay, importance classification
Safety constraints preserved at Full fidelity despite age
Chronological packing under fixed token budget: low-importance context degrades gracefully
No model retraining required

sources.title: EpiCache: Episodic KV Cache Management (arxiv)
keyInsights: Clusters conversation history into coherent episodes for topic-relevant cache compression
Episode-based retrieval during multi-turn QA
Topic-aware grouping for KV cache compression

sources.title: Keep the Cost Down: A Review on KV-Cache Optimization Methods (arxiv survey)
keyInsights: Comprehensive survey of KV cache compression spanning pre-training, deployment, and inference phases
Categories: token eviction, quantization, prefix sharing, cross-layer compression

sources.title: StreamingDialogue: Prolonged Dialogue Learning with Conversational Attention Sinks (arxiv)
keyInsights: Compresses dialogue history into 'conversational attention sinks' at end-of-utterance tokens
4x speedup over naive full-context approaches
Attention sink concept: certain tokens disproportionately attract attention and serve as compression anchors

sources.title: R3Mem: Bridging Memory Retention and Retrieval via Reversible Compression (arxiv)
keyInsights: Virtual memory tokens with hierarchical compression (document to entity-level granularity)
Reversible reconstruction: can decompress summaries back to detail when needed
Bridges the gap between aggressive compression and lossless recall

sources.title: Lilian Weng: LLM Powered Autonomous Agents (blog)
keyInsights: Memory taxonomy: sensory (embeddings), short-term (in-context), long-term (external vector store)
Short-term maps to bounded Transformer context window
Long-term via MIPS algorithms: LSH, ANNOY, HNSW, FAISS, ScaNN
Relevance + recency + importance scoring for memory retrieval prioritization
Context constraints require selective retrieval to avoid overloading attention

sources.title: LlamaIndex Chat Stores Documentation
keyInsights: ChatMemoryBuffer: configurable token_limit parameter for sliding window
SimpleChatStore: in-memory with disk persistence; default when no store provided
Remote options: Redis, Upstash, DynamoDB, Azure, PostgreSQL
Integration pattern: chat_store + ChatMemoryBuffer.from_defaults(token_limit=3000)
Optional TTL for auto-expiration of old messages
Message ordering is critical — stores maintain ordered sequences by key

sources.title: A Survey on Memory Mechanisms in LLM-Based Agents (arxiv)
keyInsights: Memory enables self-evolving agent capability for long-term interactions
Systematic survey of memory design patterns and evaluation methods
Memory supports agent-environment interactions across complex, multi-turn tasks

sources.title: LoopServe: Adaptive Dual-phase LLM Inference System (arxiv)
keyInsights: Online sparsification during prefill + progressive KV compression during decoding
Adaptive dual-phase approach for multi-turn dialogues
Production-oriented system for inference efficiency

sources.title: StreamChat: Streaming Video Understanding with Hierarchical Memory (arxiv)
keyInsights: Hierarchical memory system compressing video features for real-time multi-turn dialogue
Streaming context management pattern applicable to conversation history

sources.title: SemShareKV: Efficient KVCache Sharing for Semantically Similar Prompts (arxiv)
keyInsights: Fuzzy token matching using locality-sensitive hashing (LSH) for KV cache reuse
Cache sharing across semantically similar prompts reduces redundant computation

sources.title: REFRAG: Rethinking RAG based Decoding (arxiv)
keyInsights: Block-diagonal attention patterns in RAG contexts for 30.85x time-to-first-token acceleration
Structural sparsity in attention as basis for efficient retrieval-augmented decoding

sources.title: KV Cache Transform Coding for Compact Storage (arxiv)
keyInsights: Up to 20x compression combining PCA-based decoration, adaptive quantization, entropy coding
Transform coding approach repurposes classical signal processing for KV caches
